system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_Bmodel/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 46.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 0 has "inward-pointing faces"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1)
```

This is the inverse of the previous error. Last time `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` was inside-out, then `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` was inside-out, and now `(0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)` is inside-out again.

## Root Cause

The previous analysis suggested `hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)` would work because:
- Face (0 1 2 3) at x=-50, Face (7 6 5 4) at x=0
- Face (0 1 2) normal = (-2500, 0, 0) → -x
- Face (7 6 5) normal = (2500, 0, 0) → +x

These are opposite, so why is it still failing?

Let me re-examine the face checking. The error shows face `4(0 3 2 1)` — this is the face formed by vertices 0, 3, 2, 1. Let me compute its normal:

Vertices: 0(-50,25,25), 3(-50,-25,25), 2(-50,-25,-25), 1(-50,25,-25)
Edge 0→3 = (0, -50, 0)
Edge 0→2 = (0, -50, -50)
Cross(0→3, 0→2) = i((-50)×(-50) - 0×0), -j(0×(-50) - 0×0), i(0×0 - (-50)×0) = i(2500) - j(0) + k(0) = (2500, 0, 0)

This face is at x=-50 and its normal points in +x. For Block 0, the face at x=-50 should have normal pointing **into** the block (i.e., in the +x direction, since the block extends toward +x). The other face at x=0 should have normal pointing **out of** the block (i.e., in the -x direction).

Check the top face (7 6 5 4):
Vertex 7(0,-25,25), 6(0,-25,-25), 5(0,25,-25)
Edge 7→6 = (0, 0, -50)
Edge 7→5 = (0, 50, -50)
Cross = i(0×-50 - (-50)×50) = i(0 + 2500) = (2500, 0, 0) → +x

Both faces have normals pointing in +x? That can't be right. Let me recalculate the top face properly using vertices 4, 5, 6:

Face (4 5 6):
4(0,25,25) → 5(0,25,-25): (0, 0, -50)
4(0,25,25) → 6(0,-25,-25): (0, -50, -50)
Cross((0,0,-50), (0,-50,-50)) = i(0×-50 - (-50)×-50) - j(0×-50 - (-50)×0) + k(0×-50 - 0×0) = i(0 - 2500) - j(0 - 0) + k(0) = (-2500, 0, 0) → -x

Ah! The issue is that the face is defined by vertices (4 5 6) but the block definition uses (7 6 5 4). The face (7 6 5) is NOT the same as (4 5 6) — vertex 7 is at (0,-25,25), not (0,25,25). 

The top face of Block 0 consists of vertices 4, 5, 6, 7 — but these are NOT co-planar! Vertex 4 and 7 are at different y-values. This means the block topology is fundamentally flawed.

**The geometric problem:** The vertices don't form valid hexahedral blocks because the "top" face of Block 0 connects vertices at (0,25) to (0,-25), while the "bottom" face connects the same y-range — but the connection between vertex 3(-50,-25,25) and vertex 7(0,-25,25) is along y, not connecting to the corresponding vertex on the other side.

**Fundamental redesign needed:** The vertex layout must be restructured so that each hex block has two hexagonal faces that are planar and parallel. The current vertex placement creates tetrahedral-like or irregular hexahedra.

## Proposed Solution — Restructure the vertex layout

The square bend tutorial typically uses a different vertex arrangement than what's currently defined. Here's a proven approach from the squareBend tutorial:

**Redefine vertices** to create proper rectangular prisms. The bend should go from the x-axis direction into the -y direction. A working vertex layout would be:

```
vertices
(
    // Straight inlet section along x-axis
    ( -50  25   0 )   // 0
    ( -50  25  -0 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25  -0 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25   0 )   // 3

    (   0  25   0 )   // 4
    (   0  25  -0 )   // 5
    (   0 -25  -0 )   // 6
    (   0 -25   0 )   // 7

    // Bend corner
    (   0  25   0 )   // 8 (same as 4, inner corner)
    (  25  25   0 )   // 9
    (  25 -25   0 )   // 10
    (   0 -25   0 )   // 11 (same as 7, outer corner)

    // Straight outlet section along -y axis
    (   0 -75   0 )   // 12
    (  25 -75   0 )   // 13
    (  25 -125  0 )   // 14
    (   0 -125  0 )   // 15

    ( -500 -75   0 )   // 16
    (  25 -75   0 )    // 17 (duplicate for block connectivity)
    (  25 -125  0 )    // 18
    (   0 -125  0 )    // 19
);
```

But even this has duplicate vertices. The key principle: **each hex block must have two planar, parallel faces with consistent winding.**

**Recommended approach:** Simplify to fewer, well-formed blocks. For example, use 3 blocks:
1. Block 0: Inlet section from x=-50 to x=0 (10×10×10 cells)
2. Block 1: Bend region from x=0 to x=25, y=25 to y=-75 (10×10×10 cells) — this covers the turn
3. Block 2: Outlet section from x=25 to x=-500, y=-75 to y=-125 (100×10×10 cells)

With proper vertex ordering like:
- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)`
- Block 1: `hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)` — but only if vertices 8,9,10,11 form a proper hex face
- Block 2: `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)`

If planar faces are still problematic, consider using **`simpleBox`** or **`snappyHexMesh`** to generate the mesh, or adopt the exact vertex/block layout from the official squareBend tutorial.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error (resolves once mesh exists)

## Summary

The core issue is that the **vertex positions and/or block connectivity** don't form valid hexahedra. Each hex block requires two hexagonal (planar) faces with opposite normals. The current vertex layout creates non-convex or degenerate hex blocks. Redesign the vertices and block definitions to match the squareBend tutorial geometry exactly, ensuring all blocks pass `blockMesh` validation.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire block section with corrected vertex ordering: replace Block 0 hex definition with hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) instead of hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4); replace Block 1 hex definition with hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) instead of hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11); replace Block 1a hex definition with hex (5 10 11 6 2 15 14 13) keeping as is; replace Block 2 hex definition with hex (12 15 14 13 16 19 18 17) instead of hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19). Keep scale 0.001 and all boundary definitions unchanged.'}, {'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Remove the div(phi,K) entry from divSchemes since k is not a transported variable in this case; Remove the div(phi,Ekp) entry; Add div(phi,T) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default to divSchemes'}, {'file': 'system/fvSolution', 'changes': 'Reduce relaxation factor for p from 0.3 to 0.2; Increase relaxation factor for U from 0.7 to 0.8; Change pMinFactor from 0.1 to 0.2; Change pMaxFactor from 1.5 to 2.0; Set residual target for p to 1e-5; Set residual target for U to 1e-5; Add residual target for T of 1e-5'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Change internalField from uniform 300 to uniform 1000; Change outlet inletValue from uniform 300 to uniform 1000; Change outlet value from uniform 300 to uniform 1000'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM.
